fr.name: Transfert et dissémination des valeurs du tourisme durable portées par le réseau des montagnes emblématiques de l’arrière pays méditerranéen à travers le processus d’élaboration des éco-itinéraires mis en place dans le cadre du projet EMbleMatiC.
fr.description: Initié en 2013 via le programme Leader et étendu via le projet EMbleMatiC MED, le réseau des Montagnes Emblématiques Méditerranéennes fédère 12 des plus grandes: Olympus, Canigó, Pedraforca, Idà, Stromboli,… Ces montagnes sont situées dans les zones côtières de l'arrière-pays et partagent de fortes caractéristiques rurales ou insulaires. Ces territoires souhaitent développer un modèle de développement touristique plus responsable et durable (rééquilibrage géographique des flux de visiteurs, saisonnalité prolongée, impact environnemental réduit, retours eco accrus…). Pour ce 5ème appel Interreg MED, EMbleMatiC PLUS implique 7 partenaires (3 donneurs issus du projet EMbleMatiC & 4 nouveaux receveurs) et 7 partenaires associés en charge de la gestion des montagnes côtières ou des politiques publiques de 6 pays.Le projet EMbleMatiC PLUS a pour objectif d'approfondir et d'étendre les résultats du premier projet en transférant le «Guide de transférabilité pour la mise en œuvre d'un éco-itinéraire». Ce projet vise à: - maximiser l'impact de ce qui a été accompli et travaillé par le projet EMbleMatiC sur les nouveaux territoires - travailler ensemble pour adapter le guide de transfert initial au contexte local des receveurs et pour obtenir le meilleur plan de mise en œuvre local de l’éco-itinéraire- renforcer le réseau emblématique des montagnes méditerranéennes en rassemblant les acteurs locaux et les autorités de gestion partageant les mêmes valeurs, visions et savoir-faire.
fr.achievements: EMbleMatiC PLUS implique 7 partenaires : 3 donneurs du projet précèdent EMbleMatiC, 4 nouveaux receveurs, tous membres du réseau EMM (Emblematic Mediterranean Mountains). Ils souhaitent développer un modèle touristique plus responsable et durable dans les zones côtières, rurales de leur arrière-pays.Ainsi, EMbleMatiC PLUS vise à approfondir et à étendre les résultats du premier projet en transférant le "Guide de transférabilité pour la mise en œuvre d'un éco-itinéraire". Les éco-itinéraires déjà créés contribuent à rééquilibrer géographiquement les flux de visiteurs, à étendre la saisonnalité, à réduire l'impact environnemental tout en générant un retour économique pour ces zones à faible densité.Suite à un premier comité de pilotage, les partenaires tirèrent profit des mois d'été pour poser le cadre de gestion administrative et financière du projet et pour recruter les experts externes qui accompagnent la bonne mise en œuvre du projet.L'activité de transfert débuta dès septembre.Lors d'une première étape, les donneurs animèrent deux webinaires pour présenter le contenu du guide et les résultats précédemment obtenus.L’atelier thématique organisé en octobre dans le cadre de la première rencontre transnationale a permis aux receveurs de faire leur propre expérience de visite de deux éco-itinéraires existants au Canigó et au Pedraforca et de proposer leur propre évaluation en s'appuyant sur les 30 attributs auxquels les éco-itinéraires doivent se conformer.Dans une deuxième étape, chaque receveur a travaillé avec l'équipe de coaching pour fédérer leurs acteurs locaux et commencer à concevoir leur futur éco-itinéraire : ils ont produit 3 rapports périodiques pour justifier que leurs choix locaux de spots, d'activités, d'acteurs… étaient conformes avec la liste des 30 attributs définis dans le guide de transfert. Ce travail prépare les propositions de visites sur site que donneurs et évaluateurs viendront évaluer sur chaque territoire receveur d'ici février 2022.Les résultats de l'activité de communication sont significatifs pour ce premier trimestre.Une nouvelle affiche a été publiée pour illustrer l'identité commune emblématique du partenariat complétée par les fiches de renseignement que chaque receveur a produit en suivant les 9 critères précédemment appliqués aux donateurs.Enfin, l'avancement du projet a été correctement partagé grâce à la mise à jour du site internet et aux publications régulières sur les réseaux sociaux du projet.
fr.expected_outputs: Diagnostic de transférabilité du receveur sur la base du guide de transfert :4,Plan d'action local de mise en œuvre par le receveur de son éco-itinéraire :4
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'instruments disponibles pour améliorer le développement de tourisme durable et responsable, Nombre de destinations touristiques couvertes par un outil d'évaluation de tourisme durable, Nombre de stratégies appliquant des critères de gestion de tourisme durables, Nombre de régions et des sous-régions engagées (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de plans de tourisme durables, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

en.name: Transfer and dissemination of the values of sustainable tourism carried by the network of emblematic mountains of the Mediterranean hinterland through the process of developing eco-itineraries set up as part of the EMbleMatiC project.
en.description: Initiated in 2013 through Leader program and extended in the framework of EMbleMatiC MED Project, the Emblematic Mediterranean Mountains network is today made-up of a dozen of the greatest: Olympus, Canigó, Pedraforca, Idà, Stromboli, …All these mountains are located in hinterland coastal areas and share strong rural or island characteristics. However, these areas wish to develop a more responsible and sustainable tourism development model (geographical rebalancing of visitors flows, extended seasonality, reduced environmental impact, increased local returns…).In the framework of the 5th call for modular of the Interreg MED program, EMbleMatiC PLUS involves 3 givers partners from the previous EMbleMatiC project, 4 new receivers’ partners and 7 associated partners in charge of coastal mountains management or public policies from 6 countries around the Med.EMbleMatiCPLUS project aims to deepen & extend the first project results by transferring the “Transferability guide for the implementation of an eco-itinerary”. More specifically, this project aims to: -maximise the impact of what was accomplished & worked with the EMbleMatiC project to new Med territories,-work together in adjusting the initially produced transfer guide to fit receivers’ local context and to obtain their best eco-itinerary local implementation plan,-reinforce the Emblematic Mediterranean Mountains network gathering key local stakeholders & managing authorities sharing same values, visions & know-how.
en.expected_results: 1 Transferring conditions of the methodological guide for the implementation of eco-itineraries in new partners’ territories. : NA1 Transferring conditions of the methodological guide for the implementation of eco-itineraries in new partners’ territories. : NA
en.achievements: EMbleMatiC PLUS involves 7 partners, 3 givers from the previous EMbleMatiC project, 4 new receivers’ and 7 associated partners. These partners are members of the EMM network (Emblematic Mediterranean Mountains). They wish to adopt a more responsible and sustainable tourism development model located in their hinterland coastal areas with strong rural and island characteristics. Thus, EMbleMatiC PLUS project aims to deepen and extend the first project results by transferring the “Transferability guide for the implementation of an eco-itinerary”. These previously created eco-itineraries contribute to geographically rebalance the visitors flows, to extend the seasonality, to reduce environmental impact whilst generating economical return for these low-density areas.The summer months were put to good use to set the administrative and management framework of the project following an exhaustive and enjoyable first steering committee. Each partner recruited their corresponding external expert in readiness to carry out all activities without delay.The transferring activity started in September:During a first stage, givers run two webinars to present the contents of the guide and related past achievements.The thematic workshop held in October during the first transnational meeting, provided receivers with their own visiting experience of two existing eco-itineraries in Canigó & Pedraforca and the opportunity to articulate their own feedback relying on the 30 attributes that eco-itineraries must comply with.During a second stage, each receiver has been working with the coaching team to bring local stakeholders together and to start designing their future eco-itinerary: they produced 3 periodical reports to justify that their local choices of spots, activities, stakeholders … were complying with the list of 30 attributes defined on transfer guide. This work is preparing the on-site visit proposals that givers and assessor will come to assess on each receiver territory by February 2022.During this first semester, the communication activity produced significant results.A new poster was released to perfectly illustrate the emblematic common identity of the partnership completed with fact-sheets that receivers produced following the 9 emblematic criteria previously applied to givers’ territories.Most importantly, all activities progresses were properly shared thanks to the update of the project website and the regular postings on project social networks.
en.expected_outputs: Receiver’s transferability diagnostic relying on transfer guide :4,Receiver’s local implementation action plan for their eco-itinerary :4
en.delivered_outputs: Number of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism, Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool, Number of strategies applying sustainable tourism management criteria, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing sustainable tourism plans, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

fr.name: Multiplication et mainstreaming du modèle FIshing Tourism au niveau MED
fr.description: TOURISMED a montré que: a) la pescatourisme est une réponse concrète et durable aux défis communs de la Méditerranéenne grâce à la mise en œuvre de solutions innovantes permettant de lutter contre l'épuisement des ressources marines et le déclin de la pêche artisanale; b) le principal goulet d'étranglement pour le développement de la pescatourisme est donné par la réglementation qui ne réponde pas aux besoins réels des territoires.Le Business Model (BM) sur la pescatourisme développé par TOURISMED sera capitalisé pour promouvoir des mesures politiques efficaces favorisant la pescatourisme dans les 3 territoires receveuses (Molise, Monténégro, Bosnie), grâce à une approche axé sur la collaboration entre les décideurs publiques, les partenaires et les bénéficiaires finaux.Cette approche donnera lieu à: a) des actes politiques basés sur le BM dans les territoires receveuses; b) un protocole d’accord engageant les décideurs publics des 3 territoires receveuses; c) le renforcement des capacités des décideurs publiques pour la transférabilité et le mainstreaming du BM dans les politiques publiques locales et régionales; d) un forum des politiques publiques sur la pescatourisme.Le renforcement des capacités donnera lieu à des échanges de connaissances entre les partenaires donneurs et receveurs basés sur le BM et formalisées à travers un protocole d'accord engageant. Ça permettra l’augmentation du nombre de régions engagées dans la mise en place de plans de tourisme durable.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'instruments disponibles pour améliorer le développement de tourisme durable et responsable, Nombre de destinations touristiques couvertes par un outil d'évaluation de tourisme durable, Nombre de stratégies appliquant des critères de gestion de tourisme durables, Nombre de régions et des sous-régions engagées (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de plans de tourisme durables, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

en.name: Fishing Tourism Model Multiplied and Mainstreamed at MED Level
en.description: TOURISMED has shown that fishing tourism is a replicable answer to the common MED challenges of finding new solutions for the depletion of marine resources and the decline of the artisanal fishing sector.TOURISMED showed that the bottleneck for the development of fishing tourism is often the poor adherence of fishing tourism regulations to the real needs of territories.The fishing Tourism Business Model(BM) proposed under TOURISMED will be capitalized in this project as a theoretical basis for the creation of draft policies for fishing tourism in the receiving territories(Molise,Montenegro and Bosnia).Direct and constant interaction between the policy makers involved, project partners and beneficiaries will promote a grassroots-based policy reform starting from TOURISMED BM.This approach will lead to the following outputs:-Policy draft papers based on the TOURISMED BM in receiveing territories-MoU engaging policy makers of the receiveing territories -Capacity building for policy makers on the application of TOURISMED BM in local/regional policies-Permanent Policy Forum on Fishing tourism Institutional capacity building activities will facilitate a smooth exchange of knowledge on TOURISMED BM between givers and receivers that will be supported by a formal engagement of the policy makers in initiating the formal approval process for the draft policies that will contribute to the expand at MED level the Number of regions engaged in implementing sustainable tourism plan.
en.expected_results: 1 3 Regional/local Policy drafts based on TOURISEMD Business Model and Formal engagement of policy makers in initiating their formal approval process : The project results clearly contribute to the programme result indicator as 3 Regional/local Policy drafts in the receiving territories will be produced within the project , moreover 3 MoU will be signed between project partners and policy makers in order to formally engage them in initiating the Policy draft’s formal approval process. Additionally, concerning the associated partners other MoU will be signed in order to engage public the institution to begin a path of study and adaptation of the draft policy of the Region/municipality that for features and geographical proximity is the most similar.Moreover, the MoU will be used to formally involve the receiving partners and associated partners to the European Fishing Tourism Association already created within TOURISMED with the aim to promote the use of the businsess model. This will contribute to make the project results, and conseqently the programme indicators, more sustainable, having already experienced that the fishing tourism association is keeping alive and it is a promoter of constant upgrade of the TOURISMED results.1 3 Regional/local Policy drafts based on TOURISEMD Business Model and Formal engagement of policy makers in initiating their formal approval process : The project results clearly contribute to the programme result indicator as 3 Regional/local Policy drafts in the receiving territories will be produced within the project , moreover 3 MoU will be signed between project partners and policy makers in order to formally engage them in initiating the Policy draft’s formal approval process. Additionally, concerning the associated partners other MoU will be signed in order to engage public the institution to begin a path of study and adaptation of the draft policy of the Region/municipality that for features and geographical proximity is the most similar.Moreover, the MoU will be used to formally involve the receiving partners and associated partners to the European Fishing Tourism Association already created within TOURISMED with the aim to promote the use of the businsess model. This will contribute to make the project results, and conseqently the programme indicators, more sustainable, having already experienced that the fishing tourism association is keeping alive and it is a promoter of constant upgrade of the TOURISMED results.
en.achievements: In general terms, during the first period of TOURISMED PLUS, the planned milestones and activities have been achieved according to the foreseen timetable. The main achievements of the period can be summarised as follows:-	The project management and Steering committee rules have been produced and approved-	Communication Plan and communication material have been produced. Also, the contents of the social networks and webpage have been updated-	Capacity Building Sessions have been organised and carried out as follows:o	Corsica: 5 and 6 October o	Trabia 27 and 28 October o	Javea on 17 and 18 November-	The Stakeholder Engagement_Plan led by PETRA with the contribution of all partners has been elaborated-	Memorandum of Understanding has been drafted and although in the AF it was indicated that it would be signed by the receiving countries in October, after analysing the planning of activities it has been decided that it is more convenient to collect the signatures of the Mou after carrying out the rest of the planned activities in order to achieve a greater involvement of the local stakeholders.
en.expected_outputs: Policy draft papers based on the TOURISMED Business Model adapted to each receiveing territory :3,MoU engaging policy makers in initiating the formal approval process for the policies / territorial strategies. :3,Capacity building / Workshop for policy makers on the use of TOURISMED BM in local/regional policies :1,Permanent Policy Forum on Fishing tourism (involving the European fishing tourism association) :1
en.delivered_outputs: Number of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism, Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool, Number of strategies applying sustainable tourism management criteria, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing sustainable tourism plans, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

fr.description: Le défi transnational du projet est de transférer les connaissances et le savoir-faire en matière d'achat d'innovation développés et testés dans le précédent projet Prominent MED vers les nouveaux pays MED et les pays des Balkans occidentaux, plus spécifiquement vers les municipalités (côté demande) et côté offre par le biais de pôles d'innovation transnationaux afin de les encourager à lancer de futurs processus d'approvisionnement en innovation et à y participer et ainsi créer des opportunités de croissance commerciale. Ce projet s'appuie sur les résultats de Prominent MED tels que les orientations pour la sélection des marchés d'innovation et la stratégie de mise en œuvre des PPI dans les pays MED qui seront transférés et ajustés aux contextes spécifiques des pays bénéficiaires (Slovénie, Bosnie-Herzégovine, Grèce). Le transfert sera basé sur un processus peer-to-peer à la fois du côté de la demande et de l'offre en utilisant une combinaison d'activités conjointes. Une connaissance commune et des expériences acquises lors d'acquisitions d'innovation précédemment menées seront d'abord partagées entre tous les partenaires, puis des activités de formation et de mobilisation seront menées du côté de la demande et de l'offre. L'objectif à atteindre par ces activités est clairement visible à partir des extrants qui seront produits.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d’instruments opérationnels en faveur de l’innovation des PME, Nombre d'entreprises béneficiant de subventions, Nombre d'entreprises recevant un soutien non-financier, Nombre de clusters d'innovation transnationaux soutenus, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 1,

en.name: Public pROcureMent of INnovation boosting greEN growTh in MED area PLUS
en.description: The transnational challenge of the project is to transfer innovation procurement knowledge and know-how developed and tested in previous Prominent MED project to new MED and Western Balkans countries, more specifically to municipalities (demand side) and supply side through transnational innovation clusters in order to encourage them to launch future innovation procurement processes and participate in them and thus create opportunities for business growth. This project builds on outputs of Prominent MED such as Guidance for Innovation Procurement Selection and PPI Implementation Strategy in MED countries that will be transferred and adjusted to specific receiver countries' contexts (Slovenia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Greece). The transferring will be based on a peer-to-peer process both toward demand and supply side using a combination of joint activities. A common knowledge and experiences from previously conducted innovation procurements will be first shared among all partners and then training and mobilising activities will be conducted toward demand and supply side. The goal to be reached by these activities is clearly visible from the outputs which will be produced – Identification of institutional and regional needs, Adjusted guideline on preparing and conducting the innovation procurement process in receivers' territories and Transfer / Cooperation protocols to assure the commitment of givers' and receivers' countries and partners in the future, beyond this project.
en.expected_results: 1 International Innovation Clusters supported : PROMINENT PLUS, thanks to the transfer activities planned, will act toward the public procurers, in particular toward the municipalities of the MED and Western Balkans area in order to increase the adoption of the Innovation Procurement methodologies and tools, thus improving the quality and efficiency of public services. At the same time the project will act toward the supply side through transnational innovation clusters by conducting activities such as mobilisation, training and supporting in order to increase their knowledge of the Innovation Procurement, show them how to get involved and encourage them to participate in such public procurement processes.To the enterprises operating in transnational innovation clusters and in particular to SMEs and innovative start-ups, acting as the backbone of the key sector of the MED area, this combined action guarantees: - To ease the access to their innovative solutions- To promote the growth of the international collaboration- To enforce their business growth1 International Innovation Clusters supported : PROMINENT PLUS, thanks to the transfer activities planned, will act toward the public procurers, in particular toward the municipalities of the MED and Western Balkans area in order to increase the adoption of the Innovation Procurement methodologies and tools, thus improving the quality and efficiency of public services. At the same time the project will act toward the supply side through transnational innovation clusters by conducting activities such as mobilisation, training and supporting in order to increase their knowledge of the Innovation Procurement, show them how to get involved and encourage them to participate in such public procurement processes.To the enterprises operating in transnational innovation clusters and in particular to SMEs and innovative start-ups, acting as the backbone of the key sector of the MED area, this combined action guarantees: - To ease the access to their innovative solutions- To promote the growth of the international collaboration- To enforce their business growth
en.achievements: PROMINENT PLUS aims to successfully transfer experiences and knowledge developed in the frame of PROMINENT MED project and tested in IT, ES, PT, FR and HR to new MED countries (SI, GR, BiH) and regions with the goal to increase the involvement of innovative SME's and start-ups (through transnational innovative clusters) in public procurements using Innovation Procurement (Pre-Commercial Procurement/ Public Procurement of Innovation).In the present period the project the process started with the organization of three webinar sessions held by REAN, Consorci and Narni aimed at introducing PPI to receiving partners. Webinars are structured along the main challenges that givers encountered during Prominent MED project providing recommendations on “how to start the PPI process” to avoid common pitfalls. Following the process of evaluation of institutional and territorial needs started, coordinated by CERTH, where receivers gathers and analyse data on the national/regional legal framework of the receiver regions, identifies policy instruments supporting PPI, eco-systems, and similar cases of other PPI initiatives. In the frame of the project meeting held in Slovenia on October 25th-26th 2021 all partners transfer experiences on challenges related to the needs covering both demand and supply side and it was produced the “Identification of institutional and regional needs and innovation procurement framework” (Output 1)According with the results of the above-described process Narni and REAN started to design the Guideline on preparing and conducting the innovation procurement process, based on the Prominent MED outputs (Output 2) which will be finalized within the first trimester of 2022Under the coordination respectively of Consorci and REAN started the phase of training, guiding, and supporting stakeholders on demand and supply side, the first step was the designing of the joint syllabus intended to collect all the available knowledge resources focusing on training activities related to demand side and supply as a support for subsequent training activities. It will be also finalized within the first trimester of 2022Finally as for the training sessions all the partners decided to match the 6 training sessions (3 for the demand side and the supply side) opening the event to all the possible interested stakeholders at international level, and organize them in the frame of an international workshop which will be organized in Thessaloniki in March 2022.
en.expected_outputs: Identification of institutional and regional needs and innovation procurement framework :0,Guideline on preparing and conducting the innovation procurement process :1,Collection of PPI training and workshop materials and syllabus :0,Transfer protocols for demand and supply side stakeholders :6
en.delivered_outputs: Number of operational instruments to favour innovation of SMEs, Number of enterprises receiving grants, Number of enterprises receiving non-financial support, Number of transnational innovation clusters supported, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 1,

fr.name: Diffusion du SET - Subsidy Evaluation Tool, l’outil d'évaluation des subventions
fr.description: SISMA SET UP vise à combler l'écart entre le besoin de rénovation énergétique des bâtiments publics dans les pays de la Méditerranée et l'accès aux mécanismes financiers innovants qui rendent bancables les projets de rénovation profonde et qui minimisent les dépenses publiques.À cet effet, les résultats transférables obtenus au sein du projet SISMA comprennent le SET (outil d'évaluation des subventions) qui réalise une évaluation énergétique et économique des mesures de conservation de l'énergie et détermine le montant minimum de subvention publique nécessaire pour mobiliser des investisseurs privés.La méthodologie SET fournit aux municipalités un outil standardisé pour améliorer les compétences techniques et renforcer leur confiance dans des mécanismes de financement innovants.On va développer un programme de formation dédiée pour améliorer les compétences des destinataires et assurer le transfert des connaissances aux utilisateurs finaux, principalement les municipalités, qui peuvent aussi bénéficier d'un programme de tutorat individuel.Le paquet SET fournit des feuilles de calcul prêtes à l'emploi, des lignes directrices et des documents de formation, facilement applicables à 4 types de bâtiments publics (écoles, gymnases, bureaux et structures de soins).Étape finale du processus, les municipalités engagées signeront un accord SET, dans lequel les résultats de l'évaluation seront intégrés dans le dossier d'appel d'offres pour la rénovation énergétique des bâtiments.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification disponibles pour le suivi et la gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de stratégies en faveur des plans de gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de groupes cibles participant aux activités de sensibilisation sur l'efficacité énergétique pour les bâtiments publics, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à développer des plans/stratégies d'efficacité énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: SISMA Subsidy Evaluation Tool UPtake
en.description: SISMA SET UP aims to bridge the gap between the actual need for deep energy retrofits of public buildings across MED countries and the access to innovative financial mechanisms making deep retrofit projects bankable and minimizing public expenditure. To this aim, exploitable and transferable results achieved within SISMA include the SET - Subsidy Evaluation Tool which carries out an energy and economic-financial evaluation of ECMs (Energy Conservation Measures) and determines the minimum amount of public subsidy needed to mobilize private investors. The SET methodology provides a mature and standardized tool for local municipalities to enhance their technical skills and boost their confidence in innovative financing mechanisms. A dedicated training programme will be developed to enhance competencies and skills of ”receiver” partners and ensure the knowledge transfer to end-users, mainly local municipalities, that can also benefit from a one-to-one tutoring programme. The SET package provides ready-to-use spreadsheets, guidelines and a dedicated training documentation, which is readily applicable to four types of public buildings (schools, gyms, office buildings and health care structures). As final step of the transferring process, the involved municipalities will sign a specific SET agreement, where the results of the SET assessment are integrated in the tender documents for energy retrofitting in public buildings.
en.expected_results: 1 Increased number of local municipalities integrating the SET procedure in the tendering documentation for public building retrofits : When requiring public funding (at regional or national level) for deep energy retrofitting of municipal buildings, municipalities involved in the project will include the SET results in the technical reports attached to the application documentation. In addition, if retrofitting measures are funded directly by the municipalities, a preliminary allocation of public funding shall be done using the SET package. Therefore, the SET will be an active part of the tendering process to prioritize the allocation of public funding. Municipalities engaged in the project will sign a specific SET agreement, which formalizes their commitment to foster the uptake of the proposed methodology in local energy efficiency policies.1 Increased number of local municipalities integrating the SET procedure in the tendering documentation for public building retrofits : When requiring public funding (at regional or national level) for deep energy retrofitting of municipal buildings, municipalities involved in the project will include the SET results in the technical reports attached to the application documentation. In addition, if retrofitting measures are funded directly by the municipalities, a preliminary allocation of public funding shall be done using the SET package. Therefore, the SET will be an active part of the tendering process to prioritize the allocation of public funding. Municipalities engaged in the project will sign a specific SET agreement, which formalizes their commitment to foster the uptake of the proposed methodology in local energy efficiency policies.
en.achievements: Project activities focused on the enhancement of the SET Tool through a better fit in with local contexts, the integration of the Portuguese section in the tool as well as in the Users’ Manual and the setup of the preparatory ground for the transferring of the SET approach in the receiving countries. This entailed a feasibility check for the definition of the usage scenario of each Project country especially addressing any legal hindrance to subsidising and assessing data availability. Another crucial activity for the uptake of the SET approach was the design of the training concept and contents feeding the 2-level training programme that will be delivered to giver partners as a first step and secondly to LPAs. Giver partners will be the multipliers for the transferring of knowledge, the engagement of local end-users and for the uptake of the proposed solutions at local level. The first train-the-trainers session was held in Maribor in October 2021 and was led by GOLEA, as giver partner, and attended by ENERGAP and CEEO, as receiver partners. Inputs from receiver Partners collected in the course of the Train-the-Trainers sessions will better refine and build up the second-level training. Communication-wise, cross-cutting exchanges were going on with the MED Efficient Buildings Community which featured a capacity building workshop on SISMA PLUS SET during its Digital Days as well as with other linked EU-funded projects since SISMA PLUS is invited at Interreg Central Europe PROSPECT2030’ s final event later in 2021.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of available planning tools to monitor and manage energy consumption in public buildings, Number of strategies supporting plans on energy consumption management for public buildings, Number of target groups participating in capacity raising activities on energy efficiency for public buildings, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in developing energy efficiency plans/strategies, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,

fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d’instruments opérationnels en faveur de l’innovation des PME, Nombre d'entreprises béneficiant de subventions, Nombre d'entreprises recevant un soutien non-financier, Nombre de clusters d'innovation transnationaux soutenus, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 1,

en.name: Sharing and transferring Product Environmental Footprint experiences and methods to neighbouring countries of the Adriatic agrifood sector
en.description: Nowadays there is a huge debate on the footprinting of the agrifood supply chains and the related stakeholders have difficulties to understand their performances and how to mitigate their environmental impacts. Moreover the proliferation of environmental labels at market level is confusing the consumers when buying food products. Starting from the knowledge previously generated by the former PEFMED project in the agrifood Mediterranean countries, the general objective of the new PEFMED PLUS proposal is to replicate its culture, methodology and to transfer a set of ready-to-use outputs (National Roadmaps, PEF Wiki Platform info, Socio-Economic KPIs tools for olive oil, bottled water, wine and cheese) which will be tailored for the agrifood sector of the Adriatic basin. A number of horizontal actions such as capacity building, training and capitalisation will be matched with a series of vertical actions focused on some selected food supply chains. Each partner (giver and receiver) has a specific role and responsibility in order to:- Increase awareness about the environmental impact of traditional food productions- Deliver a "Training the trainers" programme on PEFMED former outputs - Run knowledge transfer to agrifood producers and clusters, public institution and regional authorities - Ratify Protocols and MoU with the above listed target groups in order to ensure an "after-life" plan beyond the end of the PEFMED PLUS duration including the organisation of a final event.
en.expected_results: 1 TRASNATIONAL SHARED KNOWLEDGE : Based on a preliminary research on the Adriatic "baseline" with respect to the environmental footprinting on the agrifood traditional supply chains the "Trasnational shared knowledge" will be constituted by the main following factors:1) A refined "training and capacity building" programme on how to transfer PEF and its methodology to local SMEs, public institutions and local agrifood clusters, with the support of “givers”, through cross fertilization events which will be aimed at ratifying a series of MoU with innovative agrifood clusters;A total of 45 food producers will be participating to the knowledge transfer activities organised by the receiver partners (15 per country).2) A joint communication and capitalisation plan tailored for the target groups of the Adriatic basin in order to make visible the PEFMED PLUS brand and to perpetuate the "knowledge" generated even after the end of the project at disposal of any other interested business operators including clusters and agrifood generic stakeholders, also by the promotion of 3 Protocols with policy makers recommendations and the organisation of 3 joint-events with other thematic initiatives and one Final public Conference in Split on May/June 2022.1 TRASNATIONAL SHARED KNOWLEDGE : Based on a preliminary research on the Adriatic "baseline" with respect to the environmental footprinting on the agrifood traditional supply chains the "Trasnational shared knowledge" will be constituted by the main following factors:1) A refined "training and capacity building" programme on how to transfer PEF and its methodology to local SMEs, public institutions and local agrifood clusters, with the support of “givers”, through cross fertilization events which will be aimed at ratifying a series of MoU with innovative agrifood clusters;A total of 45 food producers will be participating to the knowledge transfer activities organised by the receiver partners (15 per country).2) A joint communication and capitalisation plan tailored for the target groups of the Adriatic basin in order to make visible the PEFMED PLUS brand and to perpetuate the "knowledge" generated even after the end of the project at disposal of any other interested business operators including clusters and agrifood generic stakeholders, also by the promotion of 3 Protocols with policy makers recommendations and the organisation of 3 joint-events with other thematic initiatives and one Final public Conference in Split on May/June 2022.
en.achievements: During the first reporting period the project was mostly in line with the time schedule. In particular, the following major achievements were completed: T3.1 finalization of final report on environmental footprinting in the receivers' countries including short survey to local stakeholders;T3.2 definition of general strategy and organisation of the 1st training sessions; T3.3 elaboration of common methodology to transfer PEF method in the receivers' countries; T3.4 preparation of massive communication campaign to raise awareness on project mission addressed to public and private target groups including the finalisation of promo materials and social media accounts launch; also the project website, was opened and updated constantly and the new project brochure and roll-up draft versions were prepared. T3.5 identification of potential collaboration with other thematic initiatives especially in the Adriatic basin;T3.8 organistion of two online project meetings and the related Steering Committee meetings;T3.9 regular monitoring of project roadmap by liaising with respective task leaders.T3.10 partners were informed in due time about deadlines and templates to prepare in view of the finalisation of the 1st progress report including the collection of the FLC certificatesLast but not least, also the three Associated partners where involved at national level in local activities as dissemination and workshops (for example AP from BI was actively involved in the local stakeholder meeting organised as a side event of the 2nd SC on 22 November 2021 while ENEA organised with the italian one a stakeholder event at their premises in Sicily region on 9th December 2021).Focus on financial expendituresWith regard to financial issues 36% of the overall project budget was spent; in detail comparing the forecasted project budget for the 1s RP with the project budget spent in the 1st RP there was a small deviation of around 21.000,00 € to be added to the project budget of the 2nd RP.The deviation was caused both by the not incurred costs related to the budget line “Travel” due the pandemic situation of 2021, which did not allow to organize face to face meetings, and by the not incurred costs of the budget line “External expertise and service”, which will be incurred mostly in the 2nd RP, as planned by the project Partners.Project was presented in several events organized by the Green Growth Community in 2021 at national and international level.
en.expected_outputs: New agrifood clusters achieved by MoU :9,Agrifood SMEs reached by PEFMED PLUS tools and methods :42,National Protocols with policy makers recommendations :3,Concrete testing of PEF self-evaluation tools :3
en.delivered_outputs: Number of operational instruments to favour innovation of SMEs, Number of enterprises receiving grants, Number of enterprises receiving non-financial support, Number of transnational innovation clusters supported, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 1,

fr.name: Transfert du Modèle EduFootprint aux bâtiments publics dans les zones MED
fr.description: La stratégie 2021 pour la croissance durable et la guide sur le plan de reprise et de résilience ont identifié la rénovation des bâtiments comme une priorité pour les plans nationaux. Les fonds de reprise serviront à appliquer une stratégie claire et globale qui comprend la gestion des activités dans les bâtiments publics avec un impact négatif sur la consommation d'énergie. La Vague de Rénovations vise à améliorer la qualité de vie des personnes vivant les bâtiments, comme écoles, hôpitaux et bâtiments administratifs. BUILDING FOOTPRINT contribue à cette voie en aidant les autorités publiques à renforcer les capacités et les outils de planification des stratégies de rénovation en intégrant une approche simplifiée du cycle de vie (LCA) et les comportements des utilisateurs des bâtiments. Les donateurs entraineront et montreront aux récepteurs comment faire à mettre en œuvre Edufootprint (calculateur et APP) par un système électronique et les dirigeront à déterminer dans les édifices les domaines plus importantes afin de planifier allocations efficaces des ressources et du temps en vue de stratégies visant à générer un impact positif (sensibilisation, changement de comportement, gestion des marchés publics, optimisation des transports). En outre, les partenaires coopéreront avec la communauté «Bâtiments efficaces» pour stimuler le débat transnational et favoriser l'intégration de LCA dans la stratégie d'efficacité énergétique conformément aux récentes orientations européennes.
fr.expected_outputs: Protocole pour favoriser l'approche LCA en tant que soutien aux stratégies d'efficacité énergétique :12
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification disponibles pour le suivi et la gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de stratégies en faveur des plans de gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de groupes cibles participant aux activités de sensibilisation sur l'efficacité énergétique pour les bâtiments publics, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à développer des plans/stratégies d'efficacité énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: Transferring of EduFootprint Model to the Public Buildings in MED areas
en.description: The 2021 Annual Sustainable Growth Strategy and the Guidance on Resilience and Recovery Plans identified building renovation as a priority for national recovery plans.Recovery funds will be useful for those public authorities able to put in practice a clear and holistic strategy, which includes the management of the activities in public buildings that have environmental impacts on energy consumption.The Renovation Wave seeks to improve the quality of life for people living in and using the buildings, starting by schools, hospitals, and administrative buildings.BUILDING FOOTPRINT contributes to this pathway supporting public authorities to raise capacities and planning tools for renovating strategies integrating a simplified Life Cycle Approach (LCA) including buildings’ users behaviors.Givers will train and coach receivers to put in operation the Edufootprint products (Calculator and APP) by the e-support system, leading receivers to identify significant impact areas of public buildings and plan effective allocation of resources and time toward strategies to generate a positive impact (general awareness and behavioral change, public procurement management, optimization of transportation related services etc.).Furthermore partners will cooperate with the “Efficient Buildings” thematic community for stimulating the transnational debate and fostering the integration of the LCA into the energy efficiency strategy according with the recent European Guidances.
en.expected_results: 1 Reduction of environmental footprint by sharing best practices and monitoring measures to adapt the energy efficiency plans for public buildings : NA1 Reduction of environmental footprint by sharing best practices and monitoring measures to adapt the energy efficiency plans for public buildings : NA
en.achievements:
During RP1, IRI UL as the LP performed project & financial management activities to ensure the sound management of project, according to AF. IRI UL organized 9 project steering committees held (online, due to COVID emergency)Technical activities:A3.2 All project partners published news about EduFootprint plus project on its institutional websites and on its social media channels. LP and partners established links and contacts with HP Efficient Buildings to share project objectives and activities as well as contribute to the dissemination of knowledge about the project tools and their use throughout the Mediterranean region.A3.3: The project partners disseminated the questionnaires for users and for policy makers to their individual stakeholders. The questionnaires had the aim to acquire useful info and data about their individual stakeholders needs in terms of public buildings efficiency. The partners gathered roughly 200 questionnaires.A3.4: In accordance with the information gathered in A3.3, LP and PP1 customised EduFootprint plus tools (calculator, web app) to include the receivers' needs. A3.5: LP and PP1 set up the e-support system, which was translated to partners native languages. A3.6: LP and PP1 developed 4 webinars (available on the project’s website), then they shared them with the rest of the consortia. Afterwards the partners translated the subtitles to their national languages. A3.7: PP1 and LP set up the Educational kit, which will be vital to the final users in assisting them to understand and put inpractice the EduFootprint plus tools and approach. When finished it will be available on the project’s website. It will also be translated in the partners native languages.A3.8: The receivers worked on the testing of EduFootprint plus tools on their chosen pilot building in their area. They worked together with the relevant authorities to gather all data requested by the tool (energy consumption data and products consumption – LCA approach). Data was then inserted in the tool, and the outcomes will represent the base to improve the receivers existing energy efficiency plans.A3.9: N/A in this progress reportA3.10: COM, in cooperation with LP, contacted HP Efficient Buildings (EUROMED Network – City of Nice) to agree about the best strategy to:

1.	Design and finalize the Memorandum of Understanding. 

2.	Disseminate the MoU in other MED potential stakeholders.A3.11: N/A in this progress report
en.expected_outputs: MoU to foster the LCA approach as support for energy efficiency strategies :12
en.delivered_outputs: Number of available planning tools to monitor and manage energy consumption in public buildings, Number of strategies supporting plans on energy consumption management for public buildings, Number of target groups participating in capacity raising activities on energy efficiency for public buildings, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in developing energy efficiency plans/strategies, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,
